data.title: BNP Paribas analyzes SaaS valuations using SBC, sees upside in ServiceNow, Adobe
data.description:
BNP Paribas Equity Research analyzed software-as-a-service, or SaaS, valuations using stock-based compensation, or SBC, in the terminal value framework and noted that investors are "looking for even more bearish scenarios."

The terminal value framework calculates the estimated value of a business or asset beyond an explicit, short-term forecast period, assuming the business will continue to operate and generate cash flows indefinitely.

Analyst Stefan Slowinski said that, as software stocks have stabilized somewhat, but not yet bounced, they revisited their SaaS Smash terminal value work.

The analysts added that they ran their terminal value framework (0% terminal growth, -2% terminal growth, and zero terminal value), this time applying the analysis to SBC-burdened free cash flow. The goal is to explicitly treat stock-based compensation as a 'real' expense, reflecting the growing investor debate around whether software free cash flow is overstated when SBC is excluded, the analysts added.

"Within our SBC-burdened FCF framework, we see some valuation support across our large-cap US software coverage, assuming some residual terminal value exists," said Slowinski.

The analyst said that under this methodology, they see the most upside in ServiceNow (NOW), Adobe (ADBE), Workday (WDAY), and Intuit (INTU), with companies like Accenture (ACN) and Salesforce (CRM) pricing in some upside as well.

Slowinski sees a downside in companies that are either lower growth or carry depressed GAAP margins — Zoom Communications (ZM), IBM (IBM), and Snowflake (SNOW) — and in large-cap cyber and Electronic Design Automation, or EDA, names — Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Cadence (CDNS), Synopsys (SNPS), CrowdStrike (CRWD), and Cloudflare (NET).

"Investors are hesitant to re-engage as the sector remains susceptible to ongoing headline risk. While we have seen some confidence signals (i.e. mgmt. buying at SAP (SAP), Microsoft (MSFT), and ServiceNow, with Atlassian (TEAM) and ServiceNow (NOW) management pausing share sales), investors may view this as optics-driven especially vs. executive compensation levels; for example ServiceNow's CEO Bill McDermott's ~$3m open-market purchase vs. $113m of cumulative compensation since FY22 (NOW has not outperformed the IGV since announcement)," said Slowinski.

The analysts added that investors likely need more forceful signals.

data.title: CTO Realty Growth outlines $1.98–$2.03 core FFO guidance for 2026 while targeting expanded shopping center investments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CTO Realty Growth, Inc. (CTO) Q4 2025

Management View

  • John Albright, President and CEO, highlighted a strong fourth quarter marked by "record high leased occupancy of 95.9% same-property NOI growth for our shopping centers of 4.3% and the previously announced acquisition of a shopping center in South Florida." He emphasized the company’s focus on higher-growth Southeast and Southwest U.S. shopping centers, citing "proactive asset management and leasing" as drivers for strong results.
  • Albright noted that CTO signed leases for 189,000 square feet in the fourth quarter with "a cash rent increase of 31%," and full-year leasing activity reached 671,000 square feet with "a cash rent increase of 24%." He called attention to the leasing progress on anchor spaces, stating, "we are in active negotiations for the three anchor spaces in the Value City at Carolina Pavilion," and expects a "positive cash rent spread of approximately 60%."
  • The CEO detailed recent investment activity, including the acquisition of Pompano Citi Center for $65.2 million and the sale of The Shops at Legacy North for $78 million. He also disclosed an upcoming acquisition under contract: "a 384,000 square foot shopping center located in Texas for approximately $83 million."
  • Albright announced development plans for six outparcels, with "capital to be invested over 2026 and 2027 with leases beginning to contribute to earnings in the second half of 2027."
  • Philip Mays, Senior VP, CFO & Treasurer, stated: "For the fourth quarter, core FFO was $15.8 million, a $1.6 million increase compared to the reported in the comparable quarter of the prior year. On a per share basis, core FFO was $0.49 per diluted share compared to $0.46 per diluted share in the comparable quarter of the prior year."

Outlook

  • Mays provided guidance for 2026: "Our initial earnings guidance for the full year of 2026 is $1.98 to $2.03 for core FFO per diluted share and $2.11 to $2.16 for AFFO per diluted share." Key assumptions include "investment volume, including structured investments of $100 million to $200 million at a weighted average initial yield between 8% and 8.5%. The same-property NOI growth for shopping centers of 3.5% to 4.5%."
  • The cadence of same-property NOI growth is expected to "improve over the year as tenants included in our signed-not-open pipeline take possession of their space and commence paying rent."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, guidance is now forward-looking for 2026 with specific FFO per share and AFFO per share targets, whereas the prior quarter focused on 2025 ranges.

Financial Results

  • The company reported core FFO of $15.8 million and $0.49 per diluted share for Q4 2025. Full-year core FFO reached $60.5 million or $1.87 per diluted share.
  • Same-property NOI for shopping centers increased 4.3% in the quarter, while total same-property NOI, including noncore properties, rose 1.1%.
  • The signed-not-open pipeline stands at $6.1 million, representing 5.8% of annual cash base rents.
  • At year-end, liquidity was $167 million, including $149 million under the revolving credit facility and $18 million in cash. Net debt to EBITDA improved to 6.4x from 6.7x in the previous quarter.
  • The company repurchased $5 million of common stock in Q4, totaling $9.3 million for the year at a weighted average purchase price of $16.27.

Q&A

  • Jay Kornreich, Cantor Fitzgerald, asked about timing for rent commencement on newly signed anchor leases and unresolved vacancies. Albright explained, "we have multiple tenants buying for the space...that's going to get resolved here in the next 6 months for sure," with Mays adding that "it will ramp up about half in '26 and then they'll all be online in '27."
  • Kornreich also questioned the prospects for the New Mexico office property. Albright stated the asset is now "marketable" and the company may sell it to reinvest in open air centers.
  • Craig Kucera, Lucid Capital Markets, asked about opportunities at Pompano Citi Center and market demand. Albright identified JCPenney as a potential mark-to-market rent opportunity and said, "it's more about lease-up than taking an old tenant and bringing in a new tenant at higher rent."
  • John Massocca, B. Riley, asked about the Texas acquisition. Albright described it as "a stabilized asset with upside opportunity" including land and below-market leases.
  • Gaurav Mehta, Alliance Global Partners, inquired about changes in the signed-not-open pipeline recognition timing. Mays explained the change was driven by the sale of Legacy North and new leases signed, keeping the pipeline "significantly large."
  • Jason Weaver, JonesTrading, asked about asset allocation preferences. Albright shared, "grocer is terrific, but it's a lower-yielding kind of product," with lifestyle and power centers being current targets due to higher yields.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for greater clarity on lease-up timing, asset recycling, and acquisition strategies, expressing a slightly positive but cautious tone as they sought details on execution and portfolio growth.
  • Management remained confident in both prepared remarks and responses, frequently highlighting "strong leasing activity" and using optimistic phrases such as "we are even more excited about the future of CTO" and "the setup is really great."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained upbeat, though with increased focus on the execution timeline and asset recycling.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management shifted from discussing the closure of a specific South Florida shopping center acquisition in Q3 to announcing its completion in Q4, plus a new Texas acquisition under contract.
  • Guidance transitioned from 2025 targets to a detailed 2026 outlook, specifying AFFO and core FFO ranges, and outlining $100–$200 million in planned investment activity.
  • The signed-not-open pipeline grew from $5.5 million (5.3% of base rents) to $6.1 million (5.8%), though the recognition schedule was adjusted due to asset sales and new leases.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on leasing, pipeline recognition, and capital allocation, but Q4 included more questions on portfolio composition and mark-to-market opportunities.
  • Management’s confidence and strategic focus were consistent, with the Q4 call emphasizing execution of growth plans.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the temporary downtime from anchor tenant transitions but emphasized higher future rents and stronger tenant credits as mitigating factors.
  • The potential mark-to-market risk at Pompano Citi Center was discussed, with JCPenney identified as a tenant paying minimal rent, representing a future opportunity or risk.
  • The company flagged the possibility of near-term leverage elevation due to acquisitions, but anticipated deleveraging through select asset sales and rent commencement from leased pipeline.
  • Analysts probed for risks relating to asset sales timing, capital recycling, and market allocation, seeking assurances about execution and returns.

Final Takeaway

CTO Realty Growth delivered robust leasing and investment performance in the fourth quarter of 2025 and set the stage for increased earnings in 2026, underpinned by a sizeable signed-not-open pipeline, active capital recycling, and targeted acquisitions in high-growth markets. Management's forward guidance projects core FFO of $1.98 to $2.03 and AFFO of $2.11 to $2.16 per diluted share for 2026, reflecting confidence in proactive asset management, expanded retail leasing, and continued portfolio optimization. Investors can anticipate earnings growth as newly signed leases commence and further acquisitions and developments are realized.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: OPEN, AKAM, and more
data.description:
Stock futures erased early gains on Friday after the Supreme Court issued a landmark 6-3 ruling striking down President Trump’s sweeping 2025 "reciprocal" tariffs as unconstitutional.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) +19% - Shares jumped after Q4 revenue and adjusted EBITDA topped expectations, and the company highlighted progress in its turnaround strategy, including a 46% Q/Q jump in homes purchased, lower capital intensity, and faster inventory turnover. Opendoor is targeting positive adjusted net income on a forward 12-month basis by end-2026. However, it expects Q1 revenue to fall about 10% sequentially to roughly $662M (well below consensus) and forecasts an adjusted EBITDA loss in the low-to-mid $30M range, though contribution margin is projected to be the strongest since Q2 2024.
  • Etsy (ETSY) +6% - Shares rose after Barclays upgraded the stock to Overweight and lifted its price target to $72, pointing to improving core marketplace trends and the pending Depop sale as key turning points. While Q4 GMS and revenue were slightly below expectations, EBITDA beat estimates, and buyer trends showed early stabilization, with management guiding for year-over-year growth in every quarter of 2026. Barclays said the Depop divestment could add roughly $1.2B in cash, support buybacks, and help refocus the business on its core platform, with margins expected to exceed 28% next year.

Biggest stock losers

  • GRAIL (GRAL) -47% - Shares plunged after announcing topline results from the randomized NHS-Galleri trial, where the primary endpoint of a statistically significant reduction in Stage III-IV cancers was not met. While the study showed a favorable trend in a pre-specified group of 12 high-mortality cancers and reported meaningful reductions in Stage IV diagnoses with sequential screening, investors focused on the missed primary goal. The company said the Galleri test still demonstrated potential to shift cancer detection to earlier stages and reduce emergency presentations, and it plans further analysis with detailed data expected at the ASCO 2026 meeting.
  • Akamai Technologies (AKAM) -11% - Shares fell as weak earnings guidance overshadowed a Q4 beat. For Q1, the company expects adjusted EPS of $1.50–$1.67, below the $1.75 estimate, while revenue of $1.06B–$1.085B is roughly in line with forecasts. For FY2026, Akamai guided for adjusted EPS of $6.20–$7.20, missing the $7.29 consensus, though projected revenue of $4.4B–$4.55B came in slightly above expectations.
  • Copart (CPRT) -6% - Shares dipped after FQ2 results disappointed, showing revenue down 3.6% Y/Y to $1.12B despite pricing gains and record ASPs. Global service volumes declined 8% (3.6% ex-CAT), and U.S. insurance units fell 10.7%, with margin pressure and higher expenses fueling the negative reaction despite core operational growth.

More on related stocks:

  • Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Akamai Technologies, Inc. (AKAM) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • GRAIL, Inc. (GRAL) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Grail slides as trial misses primary endpoint, fails to show significant stage III-IV cancer reduction
  • Akamai projects 45%-50% CIS revenue growth in 2026 as AI Inference Cloud momentum accelerates

data.title: BHP, Faraday Copper explore deal to restart Arizona copper mine
data.description:
BHP (BHP) said Friday it signed a non-binding letter of intent with Faraday Copper (CPPKF) to evaluate restarting the San Manuel copper mine in Arizona and combining it with Faraday's nearby Copper Creek project to create a larger copper development hub.

Under the proposed terms, Faraday (CPPKF) would acquire BHP's (BHP) San Manuel property in exchange for a 30% interest in the equity of Faraday, as well as commensurate shareholder and marketing rights; BHP also agreed to participate in any Faraday equity raises for two years, investing up to $20M.

Operating during 1955-99, the San Manuel mine was one of the most significant copper producers in the U.S. and the largest domestic underground copper mine at the time of closure; the mine was closed in 1999 following a period of sustained low copper prices.

BHP (BHP) said it looks forward to working with Faraday (CPPKF) to "create a pathway to bring on additional U.S. copper supply to the market, [which] would support the U.S. objective of greater copper supply chain resilience."

data.title: Apparel, auto stocks rally as SCOTUS strikes down Trump tariffs
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court ruled in a 6-3 decision to strike down President Trump’s tariffs, sending shares of companies heavily dependent on foreign manufacturing higher on Friday.

The ruling found that President Trump did not have the authority to impose tariffs under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. Under the act, President Trump sought to address two foreign threats, including the influx of illegal drugs from Canada, Mexico, and China, and the “large and persistent” trade deficits.

The President determined that the drug influx had “created a public health crisis” and that the trade deficits had “led to the hollowing out” of the American manufacturing base and “undermined critical supply chains.”

While the court ruled that the president did not have the power to impose tariffs, it did address eligibility for tariff refunds.

Of the nine members of SCOTUS, dissenting opinions were written by Justices Kavanaugh, Thomas, and Alito.

The decision is having a significant impact on shares of companies that remain vulnerable to overseas manufacturing despite recent mitigation actions, including Nike (NKE), On Holdings (ONON), Victoria’s Secret (VSCO), RH (RH), Deckers Outdoors (DECK), lululemon athletica (LULU), Williams Sonoma (WSM), and automakers General Motors (GM), and Stellantis (STLA), all of which had an outsized reaction to the decision.

data.title: Wall Street moves higher as market participants assess GDP and PCE data
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages move higher on Friday as investors react to the PCE report, which came in hotter than expected.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.4%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.3%. 

From a sector vantage point, seven of the S&P 500’s 11 segments were in positive trading territory, with communication services at the top. Going the other way, energy has struggled the most so far. 

Treasury yields inched higher as the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up by 2 basis points to 3.48%, and the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up by 2 basis points to 4.09%. 

On the economic calendar, PCE inflation ticked up in December and came in higher than the consensus. The Q4 U.S. GDP (first estimate) came in at +1.4%, annual rate, vs. +2.8% consensus and +4.4% in the prior quarter.

“Friday's GDP for the fourth quarter was much lower-than-expected, but it was still positive and shows that the economy expanded at respectable clip during the final few months of 2025 even as fears about the labor market started to intensify. We expect a strong year of economic growth in 2026, driven by business investment, consumer spending and fading trade headwinds,” Rick Gardner, chief investment officer of RGA Investments noted.

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Corning Incorporated (GLW) climbed 6.1%, and shares of Intel (INTC) came down by 2.8%.

data.title: Top 10 Oil And Gas Equipment Services Stocks by Year-To-Date Performance
data.description:
Year to date, the energy sector has been one of the market’s strongest performers. In light of this, below is a list of the top 10 oil and gas equipment and services stocks ranked by their YTD performance as of February 2026.

The list features companies with market capitalizations ranging from approximately $600 million to over $60 billion, all operating primarily in the United States.

The list is topped by National Energy Services Reunited (NESR), with a YTD performance exceeding 54%. Oceaneering International (OII) and Oil States International (OIS) follow as the next highest performers, both securing gains above 47%. Tidewater (TDW), Liberty Energy (LBRT), and Helix Energy Solutions Group (HLX) round out the middle of the pack with returns between 45% and 46%.

The group features diversity in Quant ratings, with “Strong Buy” ratings for National Energy Services Reunited (NESR) at 4.98 and Forum Energy Technologies (FET) at 4.86. Industry giants such as Baker Hughes Company (BKR) and TechnipFMC plc (FTI) also appear among the top year-to-date movers, though they carry “Hold” ratings.

Here is the list:

  1. National Energy Services Reunited (NESR), YTD perf: 54.53%, Quant rating: 4.98

  2. Oceaneering International (OII), YTD perf: 50.56%, Quant rating: 3.20

  3. Oil States International (OIS), YTD perf: 47.71%, Quant rating: 4.21

  4. Tidewater (TDW), YTD perf: 46.21%, Quant rating: 3.03

  5. Liberty Energy (LBRT), YTD perf: 45.67%, Quant rating: 3.36

  6. Helix Energy Solutions Group (HLX), YTD perf: 45.14%, Quant rating: 3.09

  7. ProFrac Holding (ACDC), YTD perf: 44.47%, Quant rating: 2.31

  8. Forum Energy Technologies (FET), YTD perf: 43.52%, Quant rating: 4.86

  9. TechnipFMC (FTI), YTD perf: 38.60%, Quant rating: 3.31

  10. Baker Hughes (BKR), YTD perf: 37.37%, Quant rating: 3.22

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: Consumer sentiment lifts less than anticipated in February
data.description:
Consumer Sentiment Index: 56.6 vs. 57.3 in preliminary reading and 56.4 in January, according to the University of Michigan Survey of Consumers' final February data.

One-year inflation expectations: +3.4% vs. +3.5% in preliminary and +4.0% prior.

Five-year implied inflation: +3.3% vs. +3.4% preliminary and +3.3% in January.

Current economic conditions: 56.6 vs. 58.3 preliminary and 55.4 prior.

Consumer expectations: 56.6 vs. 56.6 in preliminary and 57.0 prior.

Developing... check back for updates

data.title: Copper fundamentals are ‘extremely good’ – Anglo American CEO
data.description:
Duncan Wanblad, CEO of Anglo American (AAUKF), says the fundamentals for copper (HG1:COM) look “extremely good” as the company positions itself to capitalize on growing demand driven by artificial intelligence, digitalization, and electric vehicles.

In an interview with CNBC, Wanblad expressed confidence in the company’s strategic direction and its heavy focus on the critical metal.

The mining giant has undergone significant portfolio reshaping to emphasize copper-focused growth.

“We are very much a copper-focused growth option for the market,” Wanblad said, noting that the vast majority of the company’s organic growth opportunities are copper-based following the combination of operations.

The optimistic outlook for copper is underpinned by several major demand drivers. AI transformation, digitalization, and the continued expansion of electric vehicles all require substantial amounts of copper, positioning the metal as central to Anglo American’s long-term strategy.

Wanblad emphasized that operational excellence and asset quality matter more than sheer size in navigating the cyclical nature of commodity markets.

“I’m not a really big believer in scale for scale sake,” he said, stressing that assets must be robust enough to survive both the peaks and troughs of market cycles.

The company also addressed a write-down of its De Beers diamond business, attributing the impairment to challenging market dynamics, including a 50% tariff on Indian products exported to the U.S.

However, Wanblad clarified that the impairment does not affect the pace or nature of the ongoing sale process, as all potential buyers are strategic players who understand the cyclical nature of the diamond industry.

Despite the complex challenges across its portfolio, Wanblad expressed confidence in the company’s progress. He noted that Anglo American (AAUKF) has made significant strides in operational excellence, portfolio reshaping, and positioning for growth, crediting the “great people” executing the strategy across all fronts.

Copper and Copper Mining ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), and (JJCTF).
